evidence_quotes.quote: the use by social media platforms (or slicing by third parties) of personalised and targeted algorithms against their victims

paragraphs.text: It is time to put in place protection against digital harassment, which has a significant impact on social media, especially on the sons of real people, with a wide variety of aspects. Some of the practices used are: — the diversion of videos (replacement of original voices, particularly in situations where it is impossible to read on the narrator’s lips) – the use by social media platforms (or slicing by third parties) of personalised and targeted algorithms against their victims, the administrators of those platforms – or criminal third parties – selecting videos systematically containing the same words (a more subtle type of harassment), placing them with some of them several times in the same thread. —action by social media platforms in the face of complaints and cumbersome procedures (e.g. obligation to refer to specific legal texts in case of behaviour deemed illegal by the user, work that should be done in collaboration between users and the social media platform concerned by the complaint) – certain frozen Instagram profiles for which it is not possible to lodge a complaint. — to better authenticate sources, for example by requiring mandatory identification, visible to the public, of authors of videos by their names and forenames, or names of companies or associations. This would encourage people to pay more attention to what they publish. — obligation for social media platforms with a European audience to have a service that can be contacted by users, with an obligation to set deadlines for replies and to postpone the number of complaints. In addition, establish a European authority (or via the Informatics and Local Freedoms Commissions) which can be contacted by all users, on which they can copy their complaint.
